title: Physics Problem-Solving: A Step-by-Step Approach
lead: Physics problems can look overwhelming. A clear process—identify what you know, what you need, and which equation connects them—makes them manageable.

sections.title: Read and List
content: Write down the given quantities with units. Write what you're solving for. Often the act of organizing the information reveals the path forward.

sections.title: Pick the Right Equation
content: Most problems use a handful of core equations. Match the variables you have and need to an equation that connects them. Draw a diagram when motion or forces are involved.

sections.title: Units Matter
content: Convert everything to SI units before plugging in. Check that units cancel correctly in your final answer. Dimensional analysis catches many mistakes.

sections.title: Check Reasonableness
content: Does the answer make sense? Negative time? A car going 500 m/s? Sanity-check your result before moving on.

cta.title: Get Physics Help
cta.text: GuruForU tutors can guide your child through physics problem-solving strategies.

faq.question: What's the first step in solving a physics problem?
faq.answer: Read and list: write down given quantities with units and what you're solving for. Organizing the information often reveals the path forward.

faq.question: How do I pick the right equation for a physics problem?
faq.answer: Match the variables you have and need to an equation that connects them. Draw a diagram when motion or forces are involved.

faq.question: Why do units matter in physics?
faq.answer: Convert everything to SI units before plugging in. Check that units cancel correctly in your final answer. Dimensional analysis catches many mistakes.

faq.question: How do I check if my physics answer is reasonable?
faq.answer: Sanity-check before moving on. Negative time? A car going 500 m/s? If it doesn't make sense, recheck your work.
